Test Case: Register with valid Full Name, Email, Password, and Role (e.g., USER), then click Sign Up".""
Expected Result: Account is created successfully; user is redirected to the login page with a success message.

Test Case: Login with valid registered Email and Password.
Expected Result: User logs in successfully and is redirected to the Home dashboard based on their role.

Test Case: Login with invalid Email or Password.
Expected Result: Login fails; an error message Invalid credentials" is displayed."

Test Case: Click Continue with Google" and authenticate via Google popup."
Expected Result: User is authenticated successfully, JWT is issued, and redirected to Home dashboard.

Test Case: As a 'USER', attempt to navigate to /admin/users via URL directly.
Expected Result: Access is denied; user is redirected to a 403 Forbidden or Home page.

Test Case: Click the Logout" button in the Navbar."
Expected Result: User session is cleared (JWT removed) and redirected to the Login page.

Test Case: As Admin, navigate to Users" page."
Expected Result: A list of all registered users is displayed with Name, Email, Role, and Status.

Test Case: Enter a valid user name or email in the search bar on the Users page.
Expected Result: The user list updates instantly to display only records matching the search query.

Test Case: Select TECHNICIAN" from the Role filter dropdown."
Expected Result: Only users with the TECHNICIAN role are displayed in the table.

Test Case: Click Add User"
Expected Result: fill details
column7: A new admin user is created and appears in the table.

Test Case: Change a user's role from USER to TECHNICIAN in the table dropdown, then click the Save button.
Expected Result: Role is updated successfully in the database, confirmed by the UI update.

Test Case: Click the Delete icon on a user row and confirm the modal prompt.
Expected Result: The user is deleted and removed from the system table.

Test Case: Navigate to Resources" page as any logged-in user."
Expected Result: List of resources (Rooms, Equipment) is displayed with current availability status.

Test Case: Select LAB" from the Category filter."
Expected Result: Only laboratory resources are displayed in the grid.

Test Case: As Admin, click Add Resource"
Expected Result: fill Name
column8: New resource is successfully added and becomes visible to all users.

Test Case: As Admin, click Edit" on a resource
Expected Result: change Capacity
column6: Resource capacity is updated successfully and reflects in the UI.

Test Case: As Admin, edit a resource and change status from AVAILABLE" to "MAINTENANCE"."
Expected Result: Status updates; the resource shows as Maintenance and can no longer be booked.

Test Case: Navigate to Bookings > Create, select a resource, choose valid Date/Time, and submit.
Expected Result: Booking is created with PENDING" status and appears in My Bookings."

Test Case: Attempt to book a resource for a time slot that is already APPROVED" for someone else."
Expected Result: System rejects the booking and shows a Time slot unavailable" error."

Test Case: Navigate to My Bookings"."
Expected Result: List of the user's personal bookings is displayed, sorted chronologically.

Test Case: As Admin, navigate to Bookings list and click Approve" on a PENDING booking."
Expected Result: Booking status changes to APPROVED; the requesting user is notified.

Test Case: As Admin, click Reject" on a PENDING booking and provide a reason."
Expected Result: Booking status changes to REJECTED; the requesting user is notified.

Test Case: Go to My Bookings and click Cancel" on an upcoming booking you created."
Expected Result: Booking is cancelled and the resource time slot becomes available again.

Test Case: Navigate to Tickets > Create, fill Title, Category, Priority, and Description, then submit.
Expected Result: Ticket is created with OPEN" status."

Test Case: Attach an image file (.png/.jpg) to the Create Ticket form and submit.
Expected Result: Ticket is created and the image is successfully uploaded and visible in details.

Test Case: As Admin, open an unassigned Ticket, select a Technician, and click Assign.
Expected Result: Ticket status changes to ASSIGNED; the assigned technician receives a notification.

Test Case: As Technician, navigate to Assigned Tickets"."
Expected Result: Only tickets specifically assigned to this technician are displayed.

Test Case: As Technician, open an assigned ticket and update status to IN_PROGRESS"."
Expected Result: Ticket status updates immediately in the system.

Test Case: As Technician, change an in-progress ticket to RESOLVED" and add resolution notes."
Expected Result: Ticket is resolved; original reporter is notified of the resolution.

Test Case: Open any ticket, type a message in the comment box, and submit.
Expected Result: Comment is appended to the ticket timeline with a timestamp.

Test Case: Have an Admin approve a booking while the requesting User A is logged in.
Expected Result: User A receives a real-time push/toast notification via WebSocket.

Test Case: Trigger a notification and view the Navbar notification bell icon.
Expected Result: The red badge displays the correct count of unread notifications.

Test Case: Open the notification dropdown and click on an unread notification.
Expected Result: The notification is marked as read, and the badge counter decreases.

Test Case: On Profile page, toggle OFF Booking Updates"
Expected Result: then have Admin approve a booking."
Status: The booking is approved, but the user does NOT receive a notification.

Test Case: As Admin, view the Home page dashboard.
Expected Result: Total Bookings, Pending Requests, Active Resources, and Open Tickets show accurate metrics.

Test Case: As Admin, view Home page when there are pending approvals.
Expected Result: The alerts panel appears, accurately displaying pending booking/ticket counts.

Test Case: As Admin, navigate to the System Analytics page.
Expected Result: Charts and graphs load and render correctly.

Test Case: As Technician, view the Home page dashboard.
Expected Result: Work summary metrics display accurately.
